content: Keith McKinney: You know, living in this post-apocalyptic world has really changed my perspective on a lot of things, especially when it comes to relationships and intimacy. It's like the old societal norms have been completely thrown out the window. Piper: I couldn't agree more, Keith. In the Commonwealth, porn and prostitution are everywhere you look. It's impossible to ignore. Keith McKinney: Exactly. And it's not just about the prevalence of it all. It's about how people view it now. There's no more shame or taboo around sex. Everyone is so open about their desires and kinks. Piper: It's a far cry from the repressed society we used to live in. I've had to do a lot of soul-searching myself, trying to understand my own desires and what I want out of my relationships. Keith McKinney: I can relate to that. It's been a journey of self-discovery for me as well. But I have to admit, there's something liberating about being able to express your sexuality so freely. Piper: Absolutely. It's like we've thrown off the shackles of our old lives and embraced a new way of living.

Piper: It's definitely a different world we're living in now, but I think it's one that allows us to be our true selves without fear of judgment. Keith McKinney: Agreed. And while there are certainly challenges and dangers out here in the Commonwealth, I'm grateful for the opportunity to explore who I am and what I want. Piper: Me too, Keith. It's been quite the journey, but one I wouldn't trade for anything. Now, let's see what other surprises this post-apocalyptic world has in store for us.
